rationale: The citing paper references Dawar and Pillutla (2000) only as part of a broad review of prior product-recall studies, including a summary row in Table 2 about brand-equity effects. It is used to illustrate existing streams in the recall literature rather than to build the paper's core lobbying hypotheses, mechanisms, or empirical design. The main theorizing and tests in the citing paper rely on institutional legitimacy and regulatory capture arguments, not on the expectation-based framework from the cited study.
